turns.text: The size of a closet with a desk and two chairs?

turns.name: THEODORE KLEIN
turns.text: Yes.

turns.name: HARRY BLACKMUN
turns.text: The situation would be different in your view if it were as large as this courtroom?

turns.name: THEODORE KLEIN
turns.text: Your Honor, I think that that would be an element, but I don't think that it is dispositive at all. I think that the coercive elements that all combined in this case added up to the situation that existed here.

turns.name: BYRON WHITE
turns.text: Well, let's assume that the officers said, we hereby arrest you, and then they asked--

turns.name: THEODORE KLEIN
turns.text: It could be an illegal arrest.

turns.name: BYRON WHITE
turns.text: --Well, let's assume there was even probable cause.

turns.name: THEODORE KLEIN
turns.text: Yes, Your Honor.

turns.name: BYRON WHITE
turns.text: And they say, may we search your suitcase, and he says yes. There is nothing wrong with that consent necessarily, is there?

turns.name: THEODORE KLEIN
turns.text: I would say that there is, Your Honor.

turns.name: BYRON WHITE
turns.text: There was probable cause.

turns.name: THEODORE KLEIN
turns.text: If there was probable cause, even... oh, if there was probable cause, no. I would say not. Under Schneckloth it would be an acceptable... it would be an acceptable standard.

turns.name: BYRON WHITE
turns.text: Even if the case turned on the voluntariness, it could be found completely voluntary.

turns.name: THEODORE KLEIN
turns.text: That's correct, Your Honor, it could.

turns.name: WARREN BURGER
turns.text: Very well. Mr. Fox, do you have anything further?

turns.name: CALVIN L. FOX
turns.text: Yes, Your Honor, I have--

turns.name: WARREN BURGER
turns.text: You have six minutes remaining.

turns.name: CALVIN L. FOX
turns.text: --Yes, Your Honor. Thank you. First of all, Your Honor, we certainly do not abandon any arguments we raise in our extensive brief, including the good faith exception. I would like to address why the voluntariness issue in this Court should be overturned, and that is, this Court's various holdings, and we quote United States versus Price that a finding of consent should not be overturned unless it is clearly erroneous. The evidence in this case is not so overwhelming that either this Court or the District Court of Appeal en banc should overturn both the panel opinion and the trial court on the question of voluntariness. The defendant just stated that this was a police room, and the witnesses so testified. In fact, Johnson was asked, "Is that an official police room. " "No, it's a closet that has shelves on one end. " "Do you have any police paraphernalia whatsoever in that room? " "No, because it's an area that we really couldn't secure very well. " The police room is something that the Third District called this room. It is not something that it was in fact. With respect to why the defendant was stopped... assuming the defendant was detained... excuse me... when he was taken to the room, he was not detained because he had American Tourister luggage, Your Honor. He was detained because of numerous facts and circumstances, not the least of which included the profile as a written indication of the officers' experience, and particularly his efforts to avoid detection as are observed by the officers, and particularly Johnson's observations of the defendant's counter-surveillance, if you will. Certainly, normal airline passengers don't conduct counter-surveillance to see if they are being observed.

turns.name: WARREN BURGER
turns.text: When did they discover that he was using a false name, at least a different name?

turns.name: CALVIN L. FOX
turns.text: The alias was discovered at the very moment of the initial contact with the defendant, Your Honor, and that alias, Elmore, and Partino, cited in our brief, Elmore said, "It would be a failure of duty had the federal agents not detained Elmore at that point to investigate him further. " Now, that is detained to investigate further, which, assuming this was a detention, that it was absolutely lawful under Elmore. Elmore also said, "Upon these facts, profile or no profile, the officers also had a right to ask the defendant if he would consent to the search of his luggage. " Patino reached exactly a similar result. With respect to the test to be applied, Your Honors, there's a two-part test here that we've argued in our brief. First of all, Terry and Mendenhall held that there is no detention unless there is physical force or a show of authority, and neither one of those circumstances was present in this case. The second--

turns.name: THURGOOD MARSHALL
turns.text: Didn't they say they were officers? Isn't that... They showed him a badge?

turns.name: CALVIN L. FOX
turns.text: --That is not a show of authority, Your Honor.

turns.name: THURGOOD MARSHALL
turns.text: Well, what is it a show of?

turns.name: CALVIN L. FOX
turns.text: It's a show of the officers not wanting to have a nervous courier react to the fact that he was about to be taken out in the Everglades and left in the trunk of an automobile. These officers identify themselves for that specific reason, that the courier realizes that he is not being trailed by somebody else.

turns.name: THURGOOD MARSHALL
turns.text: Then you must not object to the word "authority". You want to show them the authority to protect them.

turns.name: CALVIN L. FOX
turns.text: You want to show them the authority to--

turns.name: THURGOOD MARSHALL
turns.text: You want to show them authority.

turns.name: CALVIN L. FOX
turns.text: --alleviate their fear that somebody who is not lawful and who--

turns.name: THURGOOD MARSHALL
turns.text: Well, do you want to show them authority or not?

turns.name: CALVIN L. FOX
turns.text: --It is not a show of authority. Merely identifying oneself as a police officer is not a show of authority.

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: How does an officer make a show of authority?

turns.name: CALVIN L. FOX
turns.text: Pardon me?

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: How does an officer make a show of authority if he doesn't by identifying himself and showing the authorization to act as an officer?

turns.name: CALVIN L. FOX
turns.text: He does it by announcing to the defendant, you stay here, some sort of announcement, some sort of indication to the defendant.

turns.name: THURGOOD MARSHALL
turns.text: They asked him to open the baggage. Would that be a show of authority?

turns.name: CALVIN L. FOX
turns.text: Merely a request to open the bag is not a show of authority, Your Honor. This Court has so held in Schneckloth. If this Court accepts that announcement of identification is a show of authority, then we are out the window on the mere contact concept, which this Court accepted in Mendenhall.

turns.name: WARREN BURGER
turns.text: Your friend has conceded that they could have told him to open the bags immediately on the spot.

turns.name: CALVIN L. FOX
turns.text: Yes, Your Honor. His whole thing apparently rests at this point on whether or not the movement to the room is some sort of detention.

turns.name: WARREN BURGER
turns.text: Is that a show of authority or an exercise of authority?

turns.name: CALVIN L. FOX
turns.text: No, Your Honor, I think it was merely a request.

turns.name: BYRON WHITE
turns.text: Well, there still could be a difference between asking a person, may we search your luggage, and saying to him, stay right here, now, open your luggage, we are officers.

turns.name: CALVIN L. FOX
turns.text: Yes, Your Honor.

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: That's an order.

turns.name: CALVIN L. FOX
turns.text: That's a different circumstance. Exactly. That is a show of authority. Now, the second part of the--

turns.name: BYRON WHITE
turns.text: That is an exercise of coercion.

turns.name: CALVIN L. FOX
turns.text: --Stay right here, we are going to get a warrant, that certainly is an exercise--

turns.name: BYRON WHITE
turns.text: Well, or saying, now, open your luggage, we order you to open your luggage.

turns.name: CALVIN L. FOX
turns.text: --Assuming that had been held in the trial court, that would be an exercise of authority. Your Honor, there is a second part to our analysis here as to the test to be applied in this case, and that is, there must be a balancing test between the public's interest in... the compelling public interest in law enforcement and the de minimus intrusion which is present in this case, and we would urge this Court to follow the balancing test as articulated in Michigan versus Summers. Thank you.

turns.name: WARREN BURGER
turns.text: Thank you, gentlemen. The case is submitted.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: The Honorable Court is now adjourned until tomorrow at 10:00.
